sn11.9:1.0.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, once in the past a number of seers who were virtuous and of good character had settled down in leaf huts in a tract of forest. Then Sakka, lord of the devas, and Vepacitti, lord of the asuras, approached those seers.
sn11.9:1.0.2: v.898"‘The odour of the seers long bound by their vows,Emitted from their bodies, goes with the wind.Turn away from here, O thousand-eyed god,For the seers’ odour is foul, O deva-king.’
sn11.9:1.0.3: v.899"‘Let the odour of the seers long bound by their vows,Emitted from their bodies, go with the wind;We yearn for this odour, O venerable sirs,As for a garland of flowers on the head.sn.i.227The devas do not perceive it as repulsive.’"
